text: Here, in what feels like the in-between of a conference or meeting, I sense a peculiar liminality. The quiet hum of electronics and the distant glow of a presentation screen echo the temporary nature of these intellectual gatherings. I am both present and detached, observing the movements and expressions of those around me, aware of the structures that shape our interactions. There is a slight tension in the air—the anticipation that accompanies moments of pause—inviting me to wonder where my attention should rest: on the people, the ideas circulating in the room, or something subtler, unspoken, waiting to emerge.
